summary: A Go-based CLI tool called cosmo-smoke for validating, migrating, and running infrastructure smoke tests with MCP integration and Goss compatibility.
concerns: Uncommitted work (11 modified, 4 staged, 6 untracked) suggests instability
Goss migration module implies legacy dependency that may need ongoing maintenance
Monolithic internal package could become unwieldy as the codebase grows
Scattered test specs across .goralph/specs and ClaudeDesktop/specs
suggestions: Commit or stash the current working tree changes to establish a clean baseline
Consolidate test specification directories into a single location
Document the MCP handler endpoints and schema validation rules
Consider extracting the Goss migration into a standalone tool once migration is complete
